page: Hetzner Cloud cx53 is an x86_64 virtual server powered by the QEMU hypervisor. It features 16 shared vCPUs based on the AMD EPYC-Rome processor architecture running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 31.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 1.95 GB of RAM per core, and is bundled with 320 GB of local SSD storage.

In benchmark evaluations, the cx53 demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, scoring 2667.99 in stress-ng tests, which places it in the top 25% tier. Multi-core CPU performance is average, with a stress-ng score of 42844.81 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 10997.0. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show average performance, reaching 128.82 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 70.57 GB/sec for large-block writes.

The server exhibits strong text generation speeds for small language models, achieving 276.25 tokens per second with a 135M parameter model. However, prompt processing and larger model inference, such as with a 7B model, fall into the average performance tier. The shared CPU allocation presents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, non-burstable maximum CPU utilization, though it offers a resource-dense and cost-efficient profile for multi-threaded tasks that do not constantly saturate all cores.
This instance is well-suited for workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and intermediate multi-threaded processing tasks.
description: Hetzner Cloud cx53 is an x86_64 virtual server configured with 16 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 31.0 GB of RAM, and 320 GB of local SSD storage. Operating under the QEMU hypervisor, this instance delivers a balanced hardware profile with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Benchmark data highlights strong single-core CPU performance, scoring 2667.99 in stress-ng, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. The server also demonstrates strong text generation performance for small language models, reaching 276.25 tokens per second. While the shared CPU allocation introduces potential performance variability under sustained peak loads, the cx53 offers a highly resource-dense option for multi-threaded workloads. It is best suited for applications such as static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and general-purpose multi-threaded processing.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cx53 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs, 31.0 GB of RAM, and 320 GB of local SSD storage, delivering strong single-core performance for multi-threaded workloads.

tagline: A resource-dense virtual server featuring sixteen shared AMD EPYC cores, balanced memory, and local SSD storage for multi-threaded workloads.

31.0 GB of system memory and 320 GB of local SSD storage
